commit.message:
perf: one-hot Dory commits with homomorphic hint combining (#16)

* perf: one-hot batch affine additions for Dory commits

Replace dense K×T field element expansion + Pippenger MSM for one-hot
polynomials (38 of 40 committed polys) with sparse index arrays + batch
affine point additions using Montgomery's batch inversion trick.

- Add batchInversion to MontgomeryField (1 inversion + 3(n-1) muls)
- Add batch_affine.zig with batchG1Additions/batchG1AdditionsMulti
- Add commitOneHotWithPool to Dory (direct G1 basis point summing)
- Store onehot_indices ([][]?u8) instead of dense witness_polys for
  sparse polynomials, reducing memory from ~1.2GB to ~2.4MB
- Sparse joint poly construction: O(T) per poly instead of O(K×T)

All 8 programs verified against upstream Jolt verifier.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: use projective accumulation instead of naive affine adds

Replace batchG1Additions (1 field inversion per add) with projective
accumulation via addAffine (no inversions) + single toAffine at end.
For rows with ~64 points this eliminates ~63 expensive Fermat inversions.

primes_large: 33.8s → 17.4s (was 26.1s before one-hot change)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: homomorphic hint combining for Stage 8 Dory opening

Cache G1 row commitments from the initial Dory commit phase and combine
them homomorphically (Σ γ^k · rows_k[i]) using GLV scalar multiplication
instead of recomputing row commitments from scratch via full MSM in Stage 8.

primes_large: 26.1s → 15.7s (40% total speedup)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: replace batch affine with projective accumulation, extract Dory row index building

Remove batch_affine.zig and switch one-hot Dory commits to projective
accumulation. Extract buildOneHotRowIndexSlices for reuse, add
combineRowCommitmentHints for homomorphic hint combining, simplify
one-hot index assertions, add Fp12.isOne(), and add tests for batch
inversion and one-hot commit correctness.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>


---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(insight-blocks): readPlatformMetrics from raw source tables (COUNT-based growth)

tech_metrics_cache.growth_ratio is always NULL (only 1 snapshot per source).
Rewrite readPlatformMetrics to query raw source tables and compute growth from
30d/60d time-windowed counts with Laplace smoothing. Each source query runs
independently so a missing table silently excludes that source.

Sources: npm_stats, pypi_stats, github_trending, arxiv_ai_papers, jobs,
         hackernews_stories, reddit_tech_posts, stackoverflow_trends

Capture on_add hook output and send results to user via Telegram

Performance:

  veroorzaakte onnodige GPU layer promotie)
- Unused imports opgeschoond (getAllProducts, getEffectivePrice, REVIEW_COUNT)

Accessibility:
- Footer contrast: text-slate-600 → text-slate-500 (WCAG AA compliance)
- aria-hidden="true" op decoratieve SVGs in footer (sterren, hart)

commit.message:
fix: resilient embedding job — guaranteed self-chain, throttle handling, per-product recovery

Root causes of stale jobs identified and fixed:

1. getTextEmbedding() now retries on ThrottlingException with backoff
   and returns null instead of throwing (prevents batch-killing errors)
2. computeEmbeddings() uses Promise.allSettled — one product failure
   no longer crashes entire batch; failed products auto-retry next batch
3. Self-chain is GUARANTEED via inner try/catch around loop — after()
   fires even after errors (except user stop/pause or job completion)
4. Pipeline fetchNextBatch wrapped in try/catch inside Promise.all
5. Self-chain fetch has 15s AbortController timeout
6. EMBEDDING_BATCH_SIZE: 40→20 (80→40 concurrent Bedrock calls,
   staying within AWS 50 TPS limit)
7. Batch sizes: 40→20 (images), 60→40 (text-only)
8. Heartbeat TTL: 90s→180s (synced in stats endpoint)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: soporte multiplataforma para instalador Mac y Windows

- electron-builder.yml: excluye .next/node_modules/** (caché Turbopack
  con hashes que rompe el empaquetado en macOS)
- schema.prisma: agrega binaryTargets darwin y darwin-arm64 para que
  Prisma incluya binarios de Mac (Intel y Apple Silicon)
- main.js: PRISMA_QUERY_ENGINE_LIBRARY ahora detecta la plataforma
  automáticamente (win32 → .dll.node, darwin/arm64 → .dylib.node)
  y solo se aplica si el archivo existe

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: link C++ runtime for Tesseract static linking on all targets

Tesseract is a C++ library with a C API (capi.h). When FFmpeg's
configure tests linking via the C compiler, C++ symbols from
libtesseract.a are unresolved unless the C++ runtime is explicitly
linked. Add -lstdc++ (Linux/mingw) or -lc++ (macOS/llvm-mingw)
to extra-ldflags on all platforms.

commit.message:
feat: initial AI Engineering Platform

LangGraph-based autonomous engineering agent that receives feature
requests in natural language and implements them end-to-end:
  analyze codebase → design → implement → test → commit & push

Components:
- agent/: LangGraph graph with 7 nodes (setup, analyze, design,
  implement, test, fix, commit_push) + streaming entry point
- mcp_servers/: FastMCP servers for filesystem, bash, git, pytest
- web/: FastAPI + WebSocket chat UI with real-time phase streaming
- config/: central settings loading from config/.env
- main.py: entry point (--check, --agent CLI, web UI)

LiteLLM Proxy integration: claude-sonnet-4-6 (main),
claude-haiku-4-5 (fast phases), with 6 models available.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Claude invocation by using inference profile instead of model ID

Issue: ValidationException when invoking Claude Sonnet 4.6:
"Invocation of model ID anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6 with on-demand
throughput isn't supported. Retry your request with the ID or ARN
of an inference profile that contains this model."

Root cause: Claude 4.6 models require using inference profiles,
not direct model IDs.

Solution: Changed default from direct model ID to inference profile:
- Old: anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6 (direct model ID - not supported)
- New: us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6 (US inference profile - works!)

Available ACTIVE inference profiles (confirmed via aws bedrock):
- us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6 (US cross-region) ⭐ NEW DEFAULT
- global.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6 (global cross-region)
- us.anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0 (fast/cheap)
- us.anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620-v1:0 (older but stable)

Users can now use --claude-model-id with any inference profile:
  --claude-model-id global.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6
  --claude-model-id us.anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0

Updated help text to show correct inference profile format.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add tenant health check + automation execution engine

- tenant-health-check: comprehensive per-tenant health report covering
  config, integrations, notifications, AI, data, and automations.
  Mode-aware (dispatch tenants not flagged for missing calendar, etc.)
  Returns actionable issues with severity levels and fix suggestions.

  Logs every run and step to automation_runs/automation_run_steps.
  Called by other edge functions when events happen.

chore(mobile-expo): CI lint, typecheck, and build for Expo app (#318)

* chore(mobile-expo): add CI lint, typecheck, and build for Expo app

Add @forge/expo to the affected-package detection in CI, exclude it
from the monorepo-wide lint job, and add a dedicated lint-expo job
that runs lint (--max-warnings=0), typecheck, and build when Expo
code changes. Mirrors the existing lint-ios and lint-android pattern.

Resolves #96

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(mobile-expo): swap no-op build script for expo export

Replace the echo placeholder with `npx expo export --platform all`
so CI validates the full Metro JS bundle for iOS and Android,
catching broken imports and missing assets that typecheck alone
cannot detect.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): exclude @forge/expo from monorepo-wide build step

The build-and-test job runs `pnpm build` across all packages, which
now triggers `expo export --platform all` for @forge/expo. This fails
because web dependencies (react-dom, react-native-web) are not
installed. Exclude @forge/expo (and other mobile/infra packages) from
the monorepo-wide build, matching the lint exclusion pattern. The
dedicated lint-expo job handles Expo builds independently.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* revert: remove package.json build change and build exclusion

Revert the expo export build swap and build-and-test exclusion.
The package.json change is out of scope for this CI-only issue.
The build step remains a no-op echo, so no exclusion is needed.

commit.message:
Initial rag-data-firewall MVP: OPA-style policy engine for secure RAG

Firewall-ordered rules evaluate retrieval requests against chunk
sensitivity labels, trust levels, source origin, requester type,
and session trust. Actions: allow, deny, redact, require-approval.
Every decision includes full evidence chain.

Scanners: prompt injection (14 patterns), PII detection (6 types),
suspicious code patterns, composite risk scoring.

CLI: serve, ingest, scan, query, list
Daemon API on :8500 with deny-by-default policy
25 tests passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: enforce one-zombie-per-hole with per-hole spawn queue

Each ground spawn hole now tracks an occupying zombie and a queue of
pending spawns. If a hole is busy when selected, the spawn is deferred
rather than stacking multiple zombies in the same hole. The hole is
released (and the next queued zombie created) when the occupant
finishes emerging (SPAWNING→CHASING) or is killed mid-emergence, which
also prevents the round from getting stuck due to phantom alive counts.

- Add `spawnHoleId` to Zombie and `spawnQueue` to GroundSpawn types
- Add `groundSpawnById` Map for O(1) hole lookup
- Extract `_createZombieAtHole()` helper used by both initial spawn and queue processing
- Add `releaseGroundSpawnHole()` called on emergence complete and on death
- Wire ZombieAISystem to release hole on SPAWNING→CHASING transition

commit.message:
feat: enhanced progressive scroll animations across all pages

Animation System Enhancements:
- Comprehensive GSAP ScrollTrigger animations for Home and About pages
- Progressive disclosure: elements animate sequentially as user scrolls
- Multiple animation types for different content

Hero Section Animations:
- Title: Scale-up with fade (1.2s, power3.out, delay 0.2s)
- Subtext: Fade-up (1s, power2.out, delay 0.5s)
- Buttons: Bounce-in with stagger (back.out easing, delay 0.8s)
- Scale animation from 0.9 to 1 for playful effect

Section Headings:
- All h2/h3 elements fade-up individually
- 30px vertical slide with 0.8s duration
- Triggers at 85% viewport for early visibility

Section Descriptions:
- Paragraphs fade-up independently
- 20px vertical slide with 0.7s duration
- Excludes card/testimonial content

Card Animations (Enhanced):
- Staggered appearance with scale effect
- Initial: opacity 0, y 50, scale 0.95
- Final: opacity 1, y 0, scale 1
- Stagger: 0.15s between cards
- back.out(1.2) easing for bounce effect

Science Cards (Special):
- 3D rotation effect: rotationY from -15 to 0
- 60px vertical slide
- 0.2s stagger for dramatic reveal
- power3.out easing for smooth deceleration

FAQ Items:
- Slide from left (x: -40 to 0)
- 0.1s stagger for sequential reveal
- 0.6s duration with power2.out easing

CTA Buttons:
- Bounce-in from below (y: 30, scale: 0.9)
- back.out(1.5) for playful spring effect
- 0.12s stagger between multiple buttons
- Triggers at 75% viewport

Image Animations:
- Subtle zoom-out effect (scale 1.1 to 1)
- 1s duration for smooth reveal
- Applies to all images in animated sections

Stats/Numbers:
- Elastic bounce effect (elastic.out easing)
- Scale from 0.8 to 1 with 30px vertical slide
- 0.1s stagger for counting effect
- Creates attention-grabbing entrance

Location Section:
- All elements (headings, text, map) stagger in
- 40px vertical slide
- 0.15s stagger between elements
- 0.8s duration for smooth reveal

Performance Optimizations:
- GPU acceleration via transforms
- once: true prevents re-animation
- Will-change set on common classes
- Conditional animations (skip if specific animation exists)

Accessibility:
- Respects prefers-reduced-motion
- All animations disabled if user prefers reduced motion
- Smooth easing functions (no jarring effects)

Animation Timing Strategy:
- Hero: Immediate (0.2-0.8s delays)
- Sections: 75-85% viewport trigger
- Cards: 80% viewport trigger
- Creates natural flow as user scrolls

Easing Functions Used:
- power3.out: Smooth deceleration (hero, science)
- power2.out: Standard smooth (headings, text)
- back.out: Playful bounce (buttons, cards)
- elastic.out: Springy effect (stats)

Result:
- Professional, engaging scroll experience
- Each section feels alive and responsive
- Content reveals progressively
- Smooth, non-distracting animations
- Consistent timing across all pages

fix: print system — gebruik directe URLs + popup ipv broken hidden iframe/blob

Print werkte niet omdat:
- Blob URLs in hidden iframes worden geblokkeerd door browser PDF plugins
- iframe.contentWindow.print() faalt op blob PDF URLs
- print:hidden class verbergde PDF bij window.print() fallback

Fix:
- print-preview: directe API URL als iframe src (same-origin, printbaar)
- print-queue: opent print-preview als popup window ipv hidden iframe
- Queue wacht tot popup sluit, dan volgende job (sequentieel)
- Gebruiker ziet PDF preview en print dialog (visuele feedback)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Improve Claude analysis logging and fix Legacy model issue

Issue: All Claude analysis was failing with:
"Access denied. This Model is marked by provider as Legacy and you
have not been actively using the model in the last 15 days."

Changes:
1. Changed default model from us.anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022-v2:0
   to anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620-v1:0 (stable, widely available)

2. Enhanced logging visibility:
   ✓ Success: "✓ Claude analysis succeeded for: {file}"
   ✗ Failure: "✗ Claude analysis failed for {file}, using fallback summary"
   → Disabled: "→ Using simple summary (Claude disabled) for: {file}"

3. Show Bedrock errors at INFO level (not just DEBUG):
   - Users can now see "Access denied", "Model not found", etc.
   - Full stack trace still at DEBUG level

4. Log unsupported mime types:
   - Shows count of files with unsupported mime types
   - Examples at DEBUG level

5. Log when no text is extracted from a file

Now users can see at a glance:
- Which files succeeded with Claude analysis
- Which files failed and why (Bedrock error visible)
- Which files used fallback summaries
- Which files have unsupported mime types

Example log output:
  Skipping 5 files with unsupported mime types for content analysis
  ✓ Claude analysis succeeded for: Report_2024.pdf
  ✗ Claude analysis failed for: Letter.docx, using fallback summary
  Claude via Bedrock failed for Letter.docx: Access denied. This Model...

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
